headline: WARSH AT SINTRA — Fed Chair says 'AI shock is leading to a boom in capital expenditures'; supply-side expansion has 'huge implications for monetary policy' but reiterates prices 'too high'; declines to signal July FOMC action; markets fade Nasdaq futures on lack of dovish tilt
summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh made his international debut Wednesday July 1 at the ECB Forum in Sintra, sharing a panel with ECB's Lagarde, BoE's Bailey, and BoC's Macklem. Key takeaways: (a) AI framed as the dominant supply-side story — 'the AI shock is leading to a boom in capital expenditures. We see that first and foremost in demand, but I'm confident we're going to see it in supply at some point'; (b) 'Right now they're investing in the future because their expectation is the supply side of the economy will expand, and if it does, that has huge implications for monetary policy' — hint that Fed sees AI capex as disinflationary through capacity-build; (c) declined to give any July-FOMC signal but reiterated 'prices are too high'; (d) reinforced his critique of Fed 'mission creep' and exit from forward guidance. US500 opened -0.38% at 7,471 as traders had priced a more dovish tone. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE STRUCTURAL for the entire AI-infrastructure cohort — Warsh explicitly endorsed the AI-capex thesis as a monetary-policy-relevant supply-side variable, elevating hyperscaler/power/semi capex as a Fed reaction-function input; (2) NEGATIVE NEAR-TERM for high-multiple software (PLTR, NOW, CRM, SNOW, CRWD) — no rate-cut confirmation leaves the discount-rate overhang in place; (3) POSITIVE for durable AI-infra picks-and-shovels (NVDA, AVGO, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, ETN, GEV, VRT, CEG, VST) — the capex cycle is now Fed-endorsed rather than viewed as bubble excess; (4) sets up a bifurcated H2 setup: rate-sensitive software waits on cuts, AI-infra hardware/power gets structural bid.

headline: 'ONE BIG BEAUTIFUL BILL' PASSES SENATE 50-50 with VP Vance tiebreaker at ~noon ET Tuesday — Collins/Paul/Tillis defect; residential solar credit ends Dec 31 2025; utility-scale wind/solar credits phase out by 2027 placed-in-service; EV charging credit expires; nuclear/SMR credits preserved — NEGATIVE for renewables/EV cohort, POSITIVE for nuclear/gas peaker complex
summary: The Senate passed its version of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act Tuesday June 30 in a 50-50 vote with Vice President JD Vance casting the tiebreaker, after Republican Sens. Susan Collins (ME), Rand Paul (KY), and Thom Tillis (NC) joined all 47 Democrats in opposition. The bill now returns to the House for reconciliation this week ahead of Trump's July 4 signing target. Key energy provisions: (a) residential 25D solar tax credit terminates for improvements after Dec 31 2025 (accelerated from 2032 sunset); (b) utility-scale wind/solar 48E/45Y credits: projects with construction starting within one year of enactment retain full credits, all others must be placed-in-service by end of 2027 to qualify; (c) EV consumer credit already phased out; commercial EV charging credit expires June 2026; (d) NUCLEAR production tax credits (45U) PRESERVED including new-build SMRs; geothermal, hydrogen (45V) also preserved with modest paring; (e) domestic-content, prevailing-wage, and FEOC (Foreign Entity of Concern) sourcing rules tightened, disadvantaging Chinese-linked supply chains. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE for AES, NEE, and renewables developers — utility-scale solar/wind IRR compression from the 2027 placed-in-service cliff; NEE has ~40% of its 2026-2028 growth pipeline in wind/solar; (2) STRONG POSITIVE for CEG, VST, OKLO, SMR, LEU, CCJ — nuclear PTC preserved, FEOC rules push hyperscalers further toward domestic HALEU/enriched-U sourcing (Centrus); (3) POSITIVE for GEV (gas turbines), ETN (grid switchgear) — accelerated gas-peaker plus grid buildout as renewable cliff pulls forward gas/nuclear replacement; (4) MODEST NEGATIVE for BE (Bloom fuel cells), PLUG, FCEL — hydrogen credit paring pares customer economics though Bloom's SOFC gas-native pivot insulates somewhat; (5) POSITIVE READ-THROUGH for hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOGL, META, AMZN) — locked-in 20-year nuclear PPAs now enjoy PTC/production-credit certainty for the counterparty's economics; (6) X (formerly XE) uranium / TSLA (energy storage side) unaffected.

headline: Q2 2026 CLOSES AS BEST QUARTER SINCE 2020 — Nasdaq +1.52% / S&P +0.79% / Dow +0.26% Tuesday; Dow +136pts to record 52,319; S&P at 7,471 into Wednesday's US500 -0.38% open as JPMorgan's $165B pension rebalancing cleared; Warsh Sintra remarks + Senate BBB vote dominate open
summary: U.S. equity indices closed Q2 2026 with the strongest quarterly gain for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq since Q2 2020. Tuesday June 30 final session: Nasdaq +1.52%, S&P 500 +0.79%, Dow +0.26% (+136 pts) to a record 52,319.20. Semi complex outperformed with the Philadelphia Semi Index +3%+ intraday and ASML +4.44%. Wednesday July 1 pre-market: US500 opened -0.38% at 7,471 as (a) JPMorgan's flagged $165B pension/sovereign rebalance flow has now fully cleared; (b) Warsh at Sintra held a hawkish price-stability line and declined to signal July FOMC action; (c) the Senate 50-50 BBB vote at noon Tuesday remains headline overhang; (d) thin holiday-shortened liquidity into Thursday's early close and Friday July 3 NFP print. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL — the mechanical rebalance and Warsh-Sintra fade are expected end-of-quarter noise; the structural AI-capex bid is intact per Warsh's own framing; (2) semi cohort (NVDA, AVGO, AMD, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, ASML, TSM) closes H1 as the best-performing sub-sector and enters Q3 with light institutional positioning after UBS/JPM profit-taking flagged over the weekend; (3) POSITIVE momentum-carry for power/utility (CEG, VST, GEV, ETN, VRT) — Q2 was the mid-cycle re-rate quarter; H2 is the earnings-delivery quarter for the AI-power thesis with GEV Q2 print due July 23, ETN July 31, CEG August 5, VST Aug 6.

headline: SK HYNIX (000660.KS) ADR PRICED AT $166 for July 10 Nasdaq debut under 'SKHY' — $29.65B raise, 17.79M new shares (each ADR = 1/10 common); HSBC target 20% above reference; BofA / Citi / Goldman / JPM lead — most-anticipated 2026 international listing; Korean cash-in-lieu bid supportive of KOSPI memory complex
summary: SK Hynix has priced its Nasdaq ADR offering at approximately $166 per ADR ahead of the July 10 trading debut, according to bookrunner term sheets circulated Tuesday June 30. The offering: (a) 17.79 million new common shares at reference W1.92M each (~KRW 45.45T / $29.65B) — the single largest international listing of 2026; (b) each ADR represents 1/10 of a common share so the $166 ADR implies a $1,660 common-share ADR equivalent versus current Korean spot ~KRW 1.81M (~$1,320-equivalent) — ADR is priced ~26% above KOSPI last, a KOR-USD 'A-share premium' unusual for a dual listing; (c) HSBC published Friday June 26 that the fair-value target is 20% above the reference implying ~$199/ADR steady-state; (d) BofA, Citi, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan are joint bookrunners. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for 000660.KS as the dual-listing structurally widens the valuation base and adds ~$8-12B of long-only US index-fund demand at potential post-July-10 index inclusions; (2) POSITIVE READ-ACROSS for 005930.KS (Samsung) — the ADR premium re-anchors Korean memory as a globally-tradeable AI-infra asset class; (3) POSITIVE for MU as the direct HBM peer trading at a discount to SKHY implied listing multiple; (4) NEUTRAL for the memory-WFE complex (AMAT, LRCX, ASML, 8035.T, ENTG) — cap-ex plan unaffected by holding-company structure but SK Hynix's newly-raised $29B provides fresh capex capacity through 2028.

headline: COREWEAVE (CRWV) -5.1% intraday to $91.69 low Tuesday after EVERCORE ISI CUTS 2026/2027 earnings estimates; stock now -13.9% MoM after touching 52W high $187; $99B backlog (Meta $21B, Nvidia $6B, Microsoft, OpenAI) intact but Evercore models steeper margin drag from GPU depreciation and interest expense from senior notes
summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) shares slid 5.1% intraday Tuesday June 30 to a $91.69 morning low after Evercore ISI published a note cutting its 2026 and 2027 earnings estimates 'materially' following model updates for GPU depreciation schedules, hyperscaler-loan interest expense from the March/May senior notes issuance, and softer near-term utilization assumptions on the Meta $21B and OpenAI multi-billion tranches. Stock closed $96.58 Monday, -13.9% MoM after peaking at $187 in April, though still +47% YTD. Key context: (a) $99.4B revenue backlog remains intact (Nvidia $6B through 2032, Meta $21B, Microsoft ~$9.7B via IREN, OpenAI multi-billion); (b) ARIA ML-experiment analysis agent (Weights & Biases integration) launched public preview June 29 — first productized return on the $1.4B W&B acquisition; (c) Conapto Stockholm co-location partnership June 24 extends European renewable-powered AI capacity. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE for CRWV near-term as sell-side model resets flag the same GPU-depreciation-vs-contract-duration mismatch that Nebius and IREN also face; (2) NEUTRAL READ-THROUGH for MSFT, META, GOOGL, ORCL — CRWV backlog resilience implies hyperscaler AI-capex intent is unchanged; the CRWV-specific issue is unit-economics/leverage not demand; (3) MODESTLY NEGATIVE for the neocloud complex broadly (Nebius, IREN, Applied Digital) on read-across margin-compression fears; (4) NEUTRAL for NVDA — CRWV's GPU-purchase pipeline is unchanged; the multiple-compression is at the reseller not the supplier layer; (5) confirms the AI-capex trade's next-phase bifurcation: hyperscalers with balance sheets (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, META) vs. levered neoclouds face divergent H2 setups.

headline: TESLA Q2 2026 DELIVERY REPORT DUE THURSDAY JULY 2 — consensus 406,024 vehicles; BARCLAYS 418K / JPM 420K (cut from 430.5K) / RBC 405K; Austin robotaxi expanded in June, paid-miles doubled sequentially in Q1; auto volumes now 'increasingly an afterthought' as robotaxi/Optimus/AI drive the tape
summary: Tesla reports Q2 2026 deliveries on or around Thursday July 2. Wall Street consensus per LSEG stands at 406,024 vehicles; individual desk estimates: Barclays 418K (Equal-Weight, $360 PT), JPMorgan 420K cut from 430.5K on 'mixed EV demand signals' (Underweight), RBC 405K (Outperform), Cantor 'transformational 2026' base case citing AI/robotics/chips. Robotaxi progress: Austin unsupervised commercial rollout expanded through June; paid robotaxi miles nearly doubled sequentially in Q1 per the Q1 update. Full financial results including automotive gross margin, energy storage, and FSD subscription data follow after market close July 22. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL for TSLA into the print — the delivery number is now a secondary driver; the primary trade is robotaxi unit economics and Optimus/FSD progress in the July 22 call; (2) POSITIVE READ-ACROSS for TSLA's energy storage business (Megapack) — the BBB Senate vote preserves storage 45X-style credits and NEE / grid-battery demand accelerates as renewable-buildout cliff pulls forward; (3) NEUTRAL for the broader AI-hardware complex — Tesla's Dojo capex remains a rounding error vs. hyperscaler spend.

headline: TSMC (TSM) ITC PRELIMINARY RULING EXPECTED THIS WEEK on Longitude/Marlin Semiconductor 7nm patent complaint — respondents include Apple/Qualcomm/Broadcom; requested limited exclusion + cease-and-desist could bar imports of TSMC 7nm-and-below silicon; full Commission decision expected October; TSM +3.02% Monday close on relief-buying ahead of the print
summary: The U.S. International Trade Commission preliminary Administrative Law Judge determination on the Longitude Licensing / Marlin Semiconductor complaint against TSMC is expected this week (late June / early July). Complaint: TSMC's 7nm, 5nm, 4nm, 3nm and 2nm nodes are alleged to infringe five patents originating with United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC). Respondents include Apple, Qualcomm, Broadcom (AVGO) — and by extension the downstream products such as NVDA / AMD / AAPL SoCs fabricated on those nodes. The complainants seek a limited exclusion order and cease-and-desist orders covering non-x86 semiconductor devices made on TSMC's 7nm-and-below plus downstream products containing them. TSM stock closed Monday +3.02% on relief buying ahead of the print. Full Commission decision expected October 2026. Read-through to House coverage: (1) HIGH-severity tail risk for TSM, AVGO, NVDA, AMD, AAPL if the ALJ issues an adverse initial determination — full Commission overturn is possible but requires 3+ months; (2) MODESTLY POSITIVE for INTC and GFS as alternative US-fab candidates for AI SoCs during any exclusion period; (3) POSITIVE tail-risk hedge for UMC (public) and Samsung Foundry as non-infringement-affiliated capacity; (4) baseline sell-side view: patent-license settlement pre-October — no exclusion order actually enforced; the risk here is a 5-10% tape re-rate on the ALJ headline day.

headline: OPEC+ EIGHT MEETING WEDNESDAY JULY 1 — market expects rollover of Q3 output-restraint schedule; Brent $73.40 / WTI $70.10 steady; Doha US-Iran technical talks continuing without direct meeting confirmed; sensitivity for AI-infra thesis is on the Strait-of-Hormuz / power-price transmission channel rather than the immediate tape
summary: OPEC+ Eight ministerial meeting is scheduled for Wednesday July 1. Base-case expectation per Reuters/Argus flow: rollover of the Q3 production restraint schedule with token supply-restraint messaging; no incremental cuts. Brent trading $73.40, WTI $70.10 into the print. Separately, US-Iran technical delegations continue in Doha for a second day Wednesday July 1 following Tuesday's opening session — Tehran has NOT confirmed a direct bilateral meeting; per-issue disputes remain unresolved (IAEA inspections schedule, $6B frozen Qatari-bank assets release, Strait-of-Hormuz transit toll, US demand for centrifuge-enrichment cessation). Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL for the AI-infrastructure complex given oil-price channel is second-order to the electricity thesis; (2) MODESTLY POSITIVE for CEG, VST, GEV — a stable oil regime keeps gas-peaker economics predictable and supports the H2 gas-turbine order-book cadence; (3) MODESTLY NEGATIVE for AES, NEE renewable-development economics only if oil/gas fell sharply, not the base case here; (4) NEUTRAL for uranium/nuclear cohort — the SMR thesis was never crude-linked.
